steps.content: Start with $7m = 5(n + p)$. Divide both sides by $5$ to isolate the parenthesized expression: $$\frac{7m}{5} = n + p$$

choices: • "$n=\frac{5p}{7m}$": This inverts and conflates the terms; dividing $5p$ by $7m$ has no basis in the original equation. Eliminated.
• "$n=\frac{7m}{5}-p$": Dividing both sides by $5$ then subtracting $p$ gives exactly this expression. That matches the correct answer — B is correct.
• "$n=5 \left(7m\right)+p$": This multiplies instead of divides and adds instead of subtracts. Eliminated.
• "$n=7m-5-p$": This subtracts $5$ as a separate term rather than dividing by it. Eliminated.
steps.content: Subtract $p$ from both sides to isolate $n$: $$n = \frac{7m}{5} - p$$
